title: Planchonella singuliflora
taxonomy: Planchonella singuliflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This family comprises over 100 genera and 1,500 species of trees and shrubs. Planchonella singuliflora is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, specifically Fiji and Samoa. The Sapotaceae family is part of the larger order Ericales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. Within the Sapotaceae family, Planchonella singuliflora is placed in the genus Planchonella, which consists of around 50 species.

intro: Planchonella singuliflora, commonly known as the Australian plum or wild plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The Australian plum is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it beneficial for heart health and immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Planchonella singuliflora include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to have anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the Australian plum is used to make various herbal preparations, including tinctures and infusions, which are used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms associated with gastrointestinal issues.
